Industry news

Baking Paper Packaging News

Daily buyer-focused updates on greaseproof paper, baking cups, air fryer paper liners, food-contact paper packaging, PFAS-free materials and EUDR compliance.

Ask for Product SupportRead Buyer Guides

Market updates

Latest Food Paper Packaging Updates

2026-06-11Food-contact regulation

1. U.S. bill would tighten scrutiny on PFAS, BPA and phthalates in food packaging

A new No Toxics in Food Packaging Act proposal would target several chemical classes used in food packaging and processing materials, including PFAS, bisphenols and ortho-phthalates. Even before passage, it signals that large buyers will keep asking for clearer chemical disclosures.

Source: Packaging Dive
2026-05-28Cupcake liners

2. Taller cupcake liners gain attention as a simple bakery-style presentation upgrade

Consumer baking coverage is highlighting taller paper cupcake liners as a low-cost way to improve cupcake height, shape and shelf appeal. For wholesalers, the detail matters because liner height, rigidity and fit can change the finished look of the baked product.

Source: Good Housekeeping
2026-05-19EUDR

3. Paper and board suppliers prepare EUDR traceability data for December 2026

Holmen says its paper and board systems are already connected to due diligence statement workflows, with customer reference data available during the transition period. This shows how EUDR is moving from legal text into day-to-day supplier documentation.

Source: Holmen Board & Paper
2026-04-30BfR compliance

4. BfR consultation puts paper and board food-contact recommendations back under review

Germany's BfR consultation on Recommendation XXXVI covers paper and cardboard materials for food contact and remains influential because paper lacks a single harmonized EU measure. Buyers should expect suppliers to track substance listings and CAS-number details more carefully.

Source: PackagingLaw.com
2026-04-30Low-odor printing

5. Updated EuPIA guidance reinforces documentation needs for food-contact printing inks

EuPIA's April 2026 update highlights good manufacturing practice, risk assessment and supply-chain information for inks used on food contact materials. Printed greaseproof paper and cupcake liners need ink controls as well as paper compliance.

Source: European Printing Ink Association
2026-04-10Air fryer liners

6. Air fryer paper liners remain a repeat-purchase consumable through 2035

IndexBox expects air fryer paper liner demand to stay tied to appliance adoption, convenience cooking and commercial kitchen use. The market is also moving toward better sizing, stronger grease resistance, compostability claims and private-label formats.

Source: IndexBox
2026-03-11PFAS-free paper

7. EU PPWR PFAS limits make test method quality a buyer approval issue

Food Safety Magazine notes that from 12 August 2026, EU food-contact packaging must meet PPWR PFAS limit values and that defensible lab data matters near the thresholds. For paper packaging, this affects coatings, barriers and converted products.

Source: Food Safety Magazine
2026-03-01EUDR

8. EUDR forces paper, pulp and printing buyers to organize origin data earlier

EUDR preparation is pushing the paper, pulp and printing supply chain toward better raw-material origin records, certificate management and supplier due diligence. Manual document collection becomes risky as deadlines approach.

Source: osapiens
2026-02-01Baking paper market

9. Baking and cooking paper forecast supports long-term bakery packaging demand

Future Market Insights projects baking and cooking paper growth from 2026 to 2036, with bakery use, pre-cut formats and foodservice distribution among the important demand drivers. Buyers are comparing both performance and compliance as volume grows.

Source: Future Market Insights
2026-01-15Food contact paper

10. Food contact paper market growth keeps greaseproof and parchment grades in focus

Fortune Business Insights forecasts steady growth for food contact paper through 2034 across kraft, greaseproof, glassine and parchment paper. Demand is linked to takeaway, bakery, QSR, retail food and sustainability-led material shifts.

Source: Fortune Business Insights

Turn market news into a quote-ready specification

Send your product type, destination market, size, quantity and compliance requirements. LANGMAI can help match paper, coating, printing and packaging details to your buying plan.
